title: betula pubescens subsp. carpatica
intro: Betula pubescens subsp. carpatica, also known as the Carpathian birch, is a subspecies of the birch tree (Betula pubescens) native to the Carpathian Mountains in Eastern Europe. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds found in its bark. The health benefits of Carpathian birch include reducing swelling and pain, as well as treating skin conditions and wounds, due to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Carpathian birch include infusions, decoctions, and salves made from its bark, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
